response.justification: Maria Rodriguez is an accomplished full-stack and web engineer with strong TypeScript, Node.js, and event-driven architecture experience, but is fundamentally misaligned with this C++ developer role. C++ appears only peripherally in her career: a six-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project, both over a decade ago. Her entire career trajectory has been JavaScript/TypeScript, DLang, and web platforms. There is no evidence of industrial automation domain knowledge, IEC 62443 secure development practices, MQTT or industrial protocol experience (Modbus, OPC UA, DNP 3.0), or the multi-threaded systems-level C++ work this role demands.
strengths: Demonstrated experience building event-driven, high-throughput distributed systems (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Sociomantic)
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and agile practices consistently applied across roles
Breadth across backend, frontend, DevOps, and embedded domains shows adaptability and system-level thinking
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and stale — roughly six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 plus a thesis project; no modern C++11/14/17/20 or STL depth evident
Zero exposure to industrial cybersecurity, IEC 62443-4-1 SDL, or any of the required industrial protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850)
Primary skill set (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB) is entirely web-oriented and does not map to the on-premises, embedded, or industrial automation stack this role requires
